title: SCRIPT EXISTS
description: Determines whether server-side Lua scripts exist in the script cache.
complexity: O(N) with N being the number of scripts to check (so checking a single script is an O(1) operation).

returns.description: List of boolean values indicating script existence
List of boolean values indicating script existence
List of boolean values indicating script existence

params.description: One or more SHA1 digests of scripts
params.name: sha1

returns.description: Array of 1s and 0s indicating script existence
List of boolean values indicating script existence
List of boolean values indicating script existence

content:
Returns information about the existence of the scripts in the script cache. This command accepts one or more SHA1 digests and returns a list of ones or zeros to signal if the scripts are already defined or not inside the script cache. This can be useful before a pipelining operation to ensure that scripts are loaded (and if not, to load them using [`SCRIPT LOAD`]()) so that the pipelining operation can be performed solely using [`EVALSHA`]() instead of [`EVAL`]() to save bandwidth. For more information about [`EVAL`]() scripts please refer to [Introduction to Eval Scripts](). ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | | ## Return information {{< multitabs id="script-exists-return-info" tab1="RESP2" tab2="RESP3" >}} [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): an array of integers that correspond to the specified SHA1 digest arguments. -tab-sep- [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): an array of integers that correspond to the specified SHA1 digest arguments. {{< /multitabs >}}
intro:
Returns information about the existence of the scripts in the script cache.

This command accepts one or more SHA1 digests and returns a list of ones or
zeros to signal if the scripts are already defined or not inside the script
cache.
This can be useful before a pipelining operation to ensure that scripts are
loaded (and if not, to load them using [`SCRIPT LOAD`]()) so that the pipelining
operation can be performed solely using [`EVALSHA`]() instead of [`EVAL`]() to save
bandwidth.

For more information about [`EVAL`]() scripts please refer to [Introduction to Eval Scripts]().

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Returns information about the existence of the scripts in the script cache.

This command accepts one or more SHA1 digests and returns a list of ones or
zeros to signal if the scripts are already defined or not inside the script
cache.
This can be useful before a pipelining operation to ensure that scripts are
loaded (and if not, to load them using [`SCRIPT LOAD`]()) so that the pipelining
operation can be performed solely using [`EVALSHA`]() instead of [`EVAL`]() to save
bandwidth.

For more information about [`EVAL`]() scripts please refer to [Introduction to Eval Scripts]().
